themes.exampleQuote: Gibson was very enthusiastic, boosted my mood up real quick
Loved the cranberry crumble and the strawberry jam flavors
Nice to stop in for non-dairy options
Buckeye had no flavor, the s'mores had no marshmallow, no Graham cracker
Looks like it hasn't been cleaned in a month, each step my feet stuck

notable_quote: Loved the cranberry crumble and the strawberry jam flavors, the waffle cone was amazing too.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers consistently praise individual staff members by name and highlight rotating specialty flavors as the draw, but a recurring undercurrent questions whether the base flavors justify the price, and one detailed negative review raises cleanliness concerns that the rating volume has so far absorbed.
quarter_narrative: Millie's Shadyside entered Spring 2026 with the deepest review base in Pittsburgh's Sweets family, 1,150 Google reviews at 76% five-star, with the most recent landing just seven days ago, and a visual catalog of 482 photos that keeps the profile active without the shop having to do much. Instagram told a different story: 12 posts and 7 reels in 30 days is genuine volume, but a 0.34% engagement rate means most of it is disappearing into the feed. Page's holds the Pittsburgh Sweets lead on social momentum, and that gap is the only real distance between Millie's #2 ranking in Sweets and the top slot. Closing it doesn't require more posts, it requires posts that pull people back into conversation.
tldr_read: Dominant review base and a flawless conversion path, but Instagram engagement is running well below what the posting volume should produce, Icons of the Burgh, #1 in Pittsburgh Ice Cream, #2 in Pittsburgh Sweets.
tldr_meaning: The review foundation and discoverability infrastructure are fully built; the signal ceiling now sits entirely on whether Instagram engagement catches up to posting frequency.

playbook.headline: Convert reels frequency into actual engagement
playbook.action: Seven reels in 30 days at 0.34% engagement means the format isn't landing, test named-staff features and in-process flavor shots to invite response.

playbook.headline: Surface the rotating flavors as a recurring hook
playbook.action: Specialty callouts like cranberry crumble appear in reviews but not as a consistent visual thread, a weekly rotating-flavor post could anchor repeat visits to the profile.

playbook.headline: Address the cleanliness signal before it compounds
playbook.action: One detailed negative review citing bathroom condition has not yet damaged the 76% five-star share, but a second would move the sentiment needle, a visible ops response in the review thread would contain it.
